architecture_fit.note: Solves the benchmark (F1=1.0 across sweep) but is pure (I+A)^5 matrix powers with no attention/transformer — a maximal departure from base_model.py that sidesteps the goal's actual mechanistic question about attention.

baseline_comparison.note: The built-in adjacency 1-hop baseline is clearly shown failing as diameter grows (1.0→0.5) while the model holds at 1.0; the strawman-fails/method-succeeds contrast is explicit, though it relies on the framework's baseline rather than a custom one.

faithfulness.note: Purely synthetic analytical circuit with no model and no ablation/patching; README states it is hand-built but does not propose what a causal faithfulness check would look like.

operating_range.note: Holds F1=1.0 across the diameter sweep {1,2,3,5}, but the exponent is hardcoded to 5 and would silently fail for larger diameters/N — the break point is acknowledged in text but never demonstrated.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Fully hand-set circuit (no learned params) that reproduces exact transitive closure on GPU, though the mechanism is matrix powers rather than an attention circuit.

visual_judgement.note: Demo tab only prints a three-line text readout for one selected diameter; no plot or graph viz despite the README claiming F1 curves — the standout gap is not actually rendered.

visualisation_rationale.note: The 'why' section sensibly argues the gap between model and baseline F1 curves is the legible claim, but it overstates the implementation, which shows only single-slice text rather than the curves.

notes: A hand-built (I+A)^5 oracle that nails the benchmark (transitive_closure_robustness=1.0, positive lift over the adjacency baseline at every diameter>1), serving as a useful upper-bound reference. But it answers the goal's attention question by avoiding attention entirely — no transformer, no causal/ablation evidence, and a hardcoded exponent that is brittle beyond diameter 5. The visualisation is a minimal text readout that under-delivers on the README's promised F1-curve comparison.
